items.title: NARCL to buy Kurukshetra e-way debt
items.description: In a significant move towards debt resolution, the government-supported NARCL is set to take over KEPL's outstanding ₹1,500 crore debt for just ₹345 crore. Furthermore, it is expected to acquire BLA Power's ₹738 crore debt for a mere ₹285 crore.

items.title: Stock impact: 13 biggest losers in energy crisis amid raging Iran-Israel war
items.description: Oil marketing companies (OMC) stocks have seen a significant decline recently, with the shares of Hindustan Petroleum Corporation (HPCL), Bharat Petroleum Corporation (BPCL) and Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) falling up to 18% in one month. Elara said that the sharp surge in oil prices, which jumped above $100 per barrel, operates through two transmission channels: margin compression via higher COGS and earnings divergence across the oil and gas value chain. These OMCs are most vulnerable, the brokerage added.

items.title: Stock markets and geopolitical tensions: A 3-stage analysis of last 7 crashes
items.description: WhiteOak Capital Mutual Fund highlights that geopolitical crises, including the Iran–Israel war, mainly trigger short-term panic. Historical data shows markets recover quickly, with fundamentals like corporate earnings and valuations driving long-term returns. Investors are advised to stay disciplined, rebalance systematically, and treat crises as opportunities rather than reasons to sell.

items.title: D-St in Bear Trap: Sensex, Nifty log steepes drop of wartime, down 2% on oil woes
items.description: On Friday, Indian stock markets took a dramatic plunge, marking the worst single-day drop since the onset of the Gulf war. In the last fortnight, the markets shed a staggering 33.8 lakh crore in value.

items.title: Stock impact: 13 biggest losers in energy crisis amid raging Iran-Israel war
items.description: Iran and Israel fired missiles at each other as the war escalated in the Middle East, but some instead hit global markets, metaphorically. Dalal Street was no exception. Sensex and Nifty declined around 8% in just one week as oil prices surged and gas supply constraints heightened due to the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: BSE, NSE organise mock trading session today: Check timing, purpose, other details
items.description: BSE and National Stock Exchange of India are conducting a mock trading session on Saturday across equity, commodity and currency derivatives segments. The exercise will test system readiness, disaster recovery infrastructure and trading applications, while helping brokers and market participants familiarise themselves with exchange processes under simulated market conditions.

items.title: FIIs sell Indian equities worth Rs 52,704 crore in March, so far; Friday records its highest single-day outflow in 2026
items.description: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) sold Indian equities worth Rs 52,704 crore in the first half of March, with the biggest single-day outflow of 2026 recorded on Friday. The continued selling comes amid escalating geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran-Israel-US conflict and rising global uncertainty affecting investor sentiment.

items.title: Ashish Kacholia's picks: 6 stocks rally up to 120%, 4 drop up to 50% in FY26; 2 new Q3 bets revealed
items.description: Investors closely monitor the portfolios of prominent market participants on Dalal Street. In this context, ETMarkets analysed the investment holdings of well-known investor Ashish Kacholia. According to the latest data for the December 2025 quarter, Kacholia publicly disclosed stakes in about 35 stocks, with a total portfolio value of roughly Rs 2,338 crore as of March 13, 2026 — a decline of about 6% from Rs 2,477 crore in the March quarter. Notably, this list covers only those companies in which Kacholia holds more than a 1% stake and therefore may not reflect his complete portfolio.A deeper analysis shows that most of these stocks have delivered negative price returns so far in FY26. On the positive side, 10 of the 35 stocks have posted double-digit gains, with six rallying in the range of 25–120%. Among the laggards, four stocks have tumbled 40–50% in FY26. The analysis also identifies two stocks that were newly added in the December 2025 quarter. (Data Source: ACE Equity, Trendlyne).

items.title: Concurrent Losers: 12 smallcap stocks decline for 5 consecutive sessions
items.description: Over the last five trading sessions ending March 13, the Sensex benchmark tumbled 5.5%, or 4,355 points, to 74,563. The index recorded losses in four of these five sessions. During this period, about 43 stocks in the BSE Smallcap index declined in all five sessions. From this group, we highlighted 12 small-cap stocks that collectively declined by 10–25% over the same period. (Data Source: ACE Equity)"

items.title: Jefferies picks 7 stocks with up to 117% upside potential. How many do you have?
items.description: International brokerage Jefferies has refreshed its Bottom-up Analyst Top Ideas, adding seven new names to its list of 23 top buys from a coverage universe of 247 stocks. The latest picks span across sectors from banks, auto, steel, and internet. Here’s the full list of new additions. Here is everything you need to know.
